text: When fanning out read-only reviewers in parallel with a mutating tester over shared state files, warn EVERY reader about transient mutations (or sequence them) — otherwise reviewers report probe-injected state as live findings.

graphify-out/ is in .gitignore but files committed before the rule (graph.json, manifest.json, GRAPH_REPORT.md, graph.html, dated snapshots) remain tracked and churn porcelain/auto-commits on every rebuild — treat those M entries as ambient, and don't trust the "graphify-out is gitignored" doc claim for tracked paths.

Extension messages sent with deliverAs:"nextTurn" (graph-first nudge, impact-trace) are invisible to the agent within the same run — verify those branches behaviorally (e.g. the 2nd structure grep blocking proves the 1st nudged) or by jiti-loading the extension with a stub pi that records sendMessage.
